The hilt of the dag­ ger is wrapped in monIed snake skin, though none can say for certai n what type of ser pent it came from. The pomme l bears a bright (some would say flaming) red garnet. The Triad of Betrayal was created for the god Hiddukel by Ihe mastersmith-god Reorx. The Triad of Betrayal was Hiddukel 's answer to Mishakal's Circle of Love, and each of the evil artifacts fol­ lows its good counterpart. [PDF_PAGE 53 | score=6] occurred, the staff was useful to certain powerful wizards guarding the borders of Nyrond. Possession of this wooden staff allows a wizard of at least II th level to attract personal followers as a fighter of the same level. A wiza rd can use the stafT only once per year, and it must remain in the spellcaster's possession for a full month after the followers have gathered, or the troops will disband and desert. Once the month has passed, the troops will be personally loyal to the spell­ caster for one year, barrin g exceptional cond i­ tions or poor treatment . Note that this in no way affects the hirin g of men-at-arms in the norma l fashion. of the Whit e Well XP Value: 15,000 CP Value: 75

This occurs after the playing of the harp continues for one round . Those hidden are invisible even to animals and infravision , and cannot be sce nted or tracked. They can also pass withOUitrace (as the lst-Ievel druid spell), move, and speak while cloaked in silcnce, even though their speech and movements are clearly aud ible to one another.
